title: cli.py: unpack and pack commands
description: Wire `pypptx unpack <file.pptx> [output_dir]` and `pypptx pack <unpacked_dir> <output.pptx>` into `cli.py`. Each command calls the corresponding `ops.pack` function, formats output via `output_result()`, and passes `--plain` through. Errors caught at the top level are written to stderr and exit with code 1.
acceptance_criteria: `pypptx unpack deck.pptx` produces `{"unpacked_dir": "deck"}` on stdout and extracts files to that directory.
`pypptx unpack deck.pptx mydir` extracts to `mydir` and outputs `{"unpacked_dir": "mydir"}`.
`pypptx unpack deck.pptx --plain` outputs just the directory name to stdout.
`pypptx pack mydir/ out.pptx` produces `{"output_file": "out.pptx"}` on stdout.
Errors (missing file, permission denied) are written to stderr; exit code is non-zero.

handoff_summary.completed: - Added `@cli.command("unpack")` that accepts `file` (required) and `output_dir` (optional, defaults to `file.stem`), calls `ops.pack.unpack()`, and outputs `{"unpacked_dir": ...}` or plain dir name with `--plain`. - Added `@cli.command("pack")` that accepts `src_dir` and `output_file`, calls `ops.pack.pack()`, and outputs `{"output_file": ...}` or plain filename with `--plain`. - Both commands write errors to stderr via `click.echo(..., err=True)` and exit with code 1. - Syntax verified with `python -m py_compile`.
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. The `ops.pack` module already handles all error conditions (missing file, not a ZIP, missing [Content_Types].xml, etc.) and raises `ValueError`/OS exceptions that the CLI catches.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester bead should verify all six acceptance criteria end-to-end.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. Only `pypptx/cli.py` was modified; no other in-progress beads claim this file.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Defaulted `output_dir` to `Path(file.stem)` (relative to CWD at invocation time) matching the acceptance criterion `pypptx unpack deck.pptx` → extracts to `deck`. Used lazy imports (inside command body) consistent with existing `extract-text` command style.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: All six acceptance criteria map directly to testable CLI invocations; tester bead should cover: default output dir, explicit output dir, --plain for both commands, and error paths (missing file, bad zip).

conflict_risks: None. Only `pypptx/cli.py` was modified; no other in-progress beads claim this file.

execution_history.summary: Added `unpack` and `pack` CLI commands to `pypptx/cli.py`. Both commands follow the existing pattern: lazy-import the ops function, catch exceptions and write to stderr with `sys.exit(1)`, and format output via `output_result()` supporting `--plain`. The `unpack` command defaults `output_dir` to the file stem when omitted.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
